MOTION TO COMPEL ANSWERS TO INTERROGATORIES
Off Calendar Jun-01-2005 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Gary Alexander for plaintiff and moving party.
NO OTHER APPEARANCE. TENTATIVE RULING ADOPTED. The Court has read and considered the untimely opposition filed by counsel for defendant. Plaintiff's motion to compel responses to form interrogatories and request for production of documents without objections is granted. The Court finds that there was no agreement to extend the time to serve responses past March 30, 2005 and defendant has not filed a properly noticed motion for relief from waiver pursuant to CCP Sections 2030(k) and 2031(l). Accordingly, all objections have been waived. Defendant is ordered to provide further verified responses within 21 days of notice of this order. While the Court does not find that the waiver encompasses matters that may be protected by the right to privacy guaranteed by the state constitution, defendant has not identified with particularity what, if any, matters may be exempt from disclosure. The failure to provide timely responses was a misuse of the discovery process. Monetary sanctions in the amount of $941.30 are to be paid within 21 days of notice of this order. COMMISSIONER: BRUCE E. CHAN
